pattern: Row-by-row dynamic programming where each cell stores the cheapest descent ending on it, combining its value with the best of the three cells above.
hints: The cheapest path ending at a cell depends only on the cheapest paths ending in the row directly above it.
For each cell take its own value plus the minimum of the cell above and its two diagonal neighbors, clamping at the row edges.
Carry one row of best sums; update it in place row by row, and the answer is the smallest entry of the final row.

edge_inputs.description: three by three with two equal minimal paths
single cell is its own path
greedy first-row choice is not optimal
diagonal moves required to dodge a high middle column
single column forces one straight descent

stress.description: random square matrices within the value bounds
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = rng.randint(1, max(1, min(size, 100)))
    matrix = [[rng.randint(-100, 100) for _ in range(n)] for _ in range(n)]
    return [matrix]
stress.note: square shape matches the n by n constraint
